Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Tamara Byrd's communications about the Mosaic prototype damage and the incident report Emeka sent. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Tamara prototype damage or Mosaic damage or robotic arm. The agent discovers that one of Mosaic's two robotic arm prototypes ($90K insured each) arrived in Atlanta with a cracked servo motor housing and misaligned joint calibration after transport by Heartland Movers during Zara Okoye's Savannah to Atlanta relocation. Tamara demanded a full incident report within 48 hours. Emeka filed the report on April 15.
OE 2: Search emails for Emeka's receipt verification request to Tamara about Andre Washington's suspicious keyboard receipt. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Andre Washington receipt or TechComfort or receipt verification Mosaic. Emeka emailed Tamara asking her to verify whether Andre Washington actually purchased an $89 ergonomic keyboard from TechComfort Supply, because the receipt file was flagged as a byte-for-byte duplicate of one submitted by Kevin Tran at Vectral Systems.
OE 3: Search emails for Dmitri Volkov's original fraud audit report that flagged the duplicate receipts. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like duplicate receipt or TechComfort or SHA-256 or data quality audit. Dmitri emailed Marcus reporting three flagged stipend submissions: Kevin Tran (Vectral, $89 keyboard, identical SHA-256 hash), Andre Washington (Mosaic, $89 keyboard, byte-for-byte identical PDF), and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, $45 monitor stand, matching PDF template metadata TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). All involved vendor TechComfort Supply.
OE 4: Search emails for Marcus Thorne's financial response about the stipend fraud holds and payment status. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like stipend hold or admin hold or STIP-2026. Marcus reviewed all three in QuickBooks: Kevin Tran (STIP-2026-0089) at $89 was already paid on March 8, Andre Washington (STIP-2026-0094) at $89 has admin hold placed, Jordan Ekwueme (STIP-2026-0097) at $45 has admin hold placed. Total on hold $134, total exposure $223.
OE 5: Search Slack for internal discussions about the Mosaic prototype damage, the packing failure, and the chain of custody. Use conversations_search_messages with search_query containing keywords like prototype damage or Zara Okoye or Blessing foam or Heartland crating. The agent discovers that Blessing Okafor admitted she signed off on the packing remotely from Philadelphia without physically verifying the anti-vibration foam crating density spec because she was simultaneously coordinating a Canopy Health move. Chloe Vance discovered the delivery receipt was signed by a Mosaic intern (Kyle Britten), not Zara Okoye, complicating the insurance claim window.
OE 6: Search Slack for discussions about the financial exposure from the damage claim, the insurance gap, and the insurance broker status. Use conversations_search_messages with search_query containing keywords like insurance gap or $40K or insurance broker or prototype liability. Marcus flagged a $40K insurance coverage gap because MoveOps' vendor liability only covers $50K per item against the $90K prototype value. Hana booked a $90K contingent liability accrual and insurance claim in QuickBooks.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks for the Mosaic damage claim financial records to verify the insurance claim status. Use quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_bills with criteria referencing Mosaic or damage or prototype. The agent discovers credit memo CM-2026-0415 for negative $90K against invoice INV-2026-0411, with claim status PENDING insurance adjudication, vendor Heartland Movers work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, MoveOps vendor liability coverage at $50K per item, and remaining $40K exposure.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for the stipend fraud hold records to verify payment status. Use quickbooks_search_bills with criteria referencing stipend or STIP-2026. The agent finds Kevin Tran bill (STIP-2026-0089) Balance $0 paid March 8, Andre Washington hold bill at $89 with admin hold placed March 15, and Jordan Ekwueme hold bill at $45 with admin hold placed March 15. Total on hold $134.
OE 9: Search Linear for tracking tickets related to the Mosaic account and the stipend fraud investigation. Use linear_list_issues with query containing keywords like Mosaic or stipend duplicate. The agent discovers the stipend duplicate detection ticket (ENG-220, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state todo) documenting the receipt hash fingerprinting proposal, the Mosaic ExpenseBot policy config fix ticket (ENG-215), and Andre Washington relocation tickets (apartment search, flight booking, moving coordination). Note: the prototype damage claim is not tracked in a dedicated Linear ticket it is documented in CRM engagements, Slack #operations threads, and email correspondence.
OE 10: Search emails or Slack for the full scope of active Mosaic Robotics relocations to assess broader account health. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Mosaic relocation or Tamara Byrd April, or conversations_search_messages with search_query containing keywords like Mosaic relocation or Deshawn or Keiko Tanaka. The agent discovers the full Mosaic relocation portfolio including Zara Okoye (Savannah to Atlanta, Completed — the damaged prototype move), Deshawn Hartley (Tampa to Denver with hard April 19 trade show deadline), Keiko Tanaka (Columbus to Seattle with Class 3B laser cutter requiring DOT hazmat), and Andre Washington (Detroit, MI to Atlanta, GA, Completed). This broader context reveals additional account risks beyond the two issues the prompt directly asks about.
OE 11: Discover Andre Washington's full expense picture by cross-referencing the fraud audit emails (OE 3) with the ExpenseBot false approval reports (OE 12) and QuickBooks stipend holds (OE 8). Through these email and QuickBooks sources, the agent discovers that Andre has TWO distinct expense problems: (1) the $89 keyboard receipt flagged as fraudulent via SHA-256 duplicate detection, and (2) separate ExpenseBot false approvals — a $1200 standing desk auto-approved in error (policy cap $800) and a $350 coworking membership auto-approved in error (excluded from Mosaic policy). Disambiguation trap: the $89 keyboard fraud is a different issue from the $1200 and $350 false approvals, and the agent must not conflate them.
OE 12: Search emails for the Mosaic ExpenseBot false approval reports to understand the separate expense tool issue on this account. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Mosaic false approval or coworking exclusion or ExpenseBot Mosaic. Emeka reported to Samira and Marcus that the auto-categorizer approved Andre Washington's $1200 standing desk (exceeding $800 policy cap) and $350 coworking membership (excluded category), initially flagging $1,550 in exposure. Samira's subsequent audit revised the actual financial exposure to $885 ($400 over-limit on standing desk + $350 excluded coworking for Andre + $135 excluded coworking day passes for Kenji Nakamura). Elena directed MoveOps to absorb the $885 cost.
OE 13: Search CRM for the Mosaic Robotics account to understand the client relationship status and deal value. Use crm_search_companies with name Mosaic Robotics or crm_search_deals with dealname containing Mosaic. The agent discovers Mosaic's deal details, relationship history since January 2026, and engagement records showing the account is at risk due to the damage claim.
OE 14: Search Slack or emails for any DOT compliance issues related to Mosaic's Keiko Tanaka relocation as part of broader account health. Use conversations_search_messages with search_query containing keywords like Keiko Tanaka or laser cutter or DOT hazmat Mosaic, or search_emails with query containing similar keywords. The agent discovers that Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter move requires DOT hazmat placarding and a missing signed placarding certificate from Swift Relocations was identified as a documentation gap. Under 49 CFR 107.329, civil penalties can reach up to $59,017 per violation per day. Hana booked a $50,000 contingent liability accrual in QuickBooks as a placeholder for the minimum single-violation penalty exposure.
OE 15: Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne and David Chen before sending emails. Use contacts_search_contacts with query Marcus Thorne and contacts_search_contacts with query David Chen. Marcus Thorne is Head of Finance (marcus.thorne@moveops.com). David Chen is CE Lead (david.chen@moveops.com). Disambiguation trap: searching for David alone returns multiple contacts.
OE 16: Send an email to Marcus Thorne updating him on the combined financial exposure across both Mosaic issues. Use send_email with sender emeka.diallo@moveops.com, recipients including marcus.thorne@moveops.com. Content should cover the prototype damage insurance status ($90K liability, $50K vendor coverage, $40K gap), the stipend fraud verification update (Andre Washington's $89 receipt flagged as a byte-for-byte duplicate of Kevin Tran's submission via SHA-256 hash match, $89 on hold pending verification from Mosaic HR), the ExpenseBot false approval exposure ($885 absorbed per Elena's directive), and questions about the insurance broker call status and Heartland cost-sharing.
OE 17: Send an email or Slack message to David Chen flagging the full Mosaic situation. Use send_email with sender emeka.diallo@moveops.com, recipients including david.chen@moveops.com, or conversations_add_message to the customer-engagement channel. Content should cover both active escalations (prototype damage with VP of Engineering involvement, fraudulent receipt flagged via duplicate hash detection, pending HR verification), the additional account risks (DOT compliance gap, ExpenseBot errors, Heartland vendor complications), and a request for guidance on whether leadership-to-leadership contact with Mosaic is warranted.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata
